verses.latin_vulgate: Post quinque autem dies descendit princeps sacerdotum Ananias, cum senioribus quibusdam, et Tertullo quodam oratore, qui adierunt præsidem adversus Paulum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ πέντε ἡμέρας κατέβη ὁ ἀρχιερεὺς ἁνανίας μετὰ τῶν πρεσβυτέρων καὶ ῥήτορος τερτύλλου τινός οἵτινες ἐνεφάνισαν τῷ ἡγεμόνι κατὰ τοῦ παύλου
verses.king_james: And after five days Ananias the high priest descended with the elders, and *with* a certain orator *named* Tertullus, who informed the governor against Paul.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et citato Paulo cœpit accusare Tertullus, dicens: Cum in multa pace agamus per te, et multa corrigantur per tuam providentiam,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κληθέντος δὲ αὐτοῦ ἤρξατο κατηγορεῖν ὁ τέρτυλλος λέγων, πολλῆς εἰρήνης τυγχάνοντες διὰ σοῦ καὶ κατορθωμάτων γινομένων τῷ ἔθνει τούτῳ διὰ τῆς σῆς προνοίας
verses.king_james: And when he was called forth, Tertullus began to accuse *him*, saying, Seeing that by thee we enjoy great quietness, and that very worthy deeds are done unto this nation by thy providence,

verses.latin_vulgate: semper et ubique suscipimus, optime Felix, cum omni gratiarum actione.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντῃ τε καὶ πανταχοῦ ἀποδεχόμεθα κράτιστε φῆλιξ μετὰ πάσης εὐχαριστίας
verses.king_james: We accept *it* always, and in all places, most noble Felix, with all thankfulness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne diutius autem te protraham, oro, breviter audias nos pro tua clementia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα δὲ μὴ ἐπὶ πλεῖόν σε ἐγκόπτω παρακαλῶ ἀκοῦσαί σε ἡμῶν συντόμως τῇ σῇ ἐπιεικείᾳ
verses.king_james: Notwithstanding, that I be not further tedious unto thee, I pray thee that thou wouldest hear us of thy clemency a few words.

verses.latin_vulgate: Invenimus hunc hominem pestiferum, et concitantem seditiones omnibus Judæis in universo orbe, et auctorem seditionis sectæ Nazarenorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὑρόντες γὰρ τὸν ἄνδρα τοῦτον λοιμὸν καὶ κινοῦντα στάσιν πᾶσιν τοῖς ἰουδαίοις τοῖς κατὰ τὴν οἰκουμένην πρωτοστάτην τε τῆς τῶν ναζωραίων αἱρέσεως
verses.king_james: For we have found this man a pestilent *fellow*, and a mover of sedition among all the Jews throughout the world, and a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui etiam templum violare conatus est, quem et apprehensum voluimus secundum legem nostram judicare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς καὶ τὸ ἱερὸν ἐπείρασεν βεβηλῶσαι ὃν καὶ ἐκρατήσαμεν καὶ κατά τόν ἡμέτερον νόμον ἠθελήσαμεν κρίνειν
verses.king_james: Who also hath gone about to profane the temple: whom we took, and would have judged according to our law.

verses.latin_vulgate: Superveniens autem tribunus Lysias, cum vi magna eripuit eum de manibus nostris,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρελθὼν δὲ λυσίας ὁ χιλιαρχος μετὰ πολλῆς βίας ἐκ τῶν χειρῶν ἡμῶν ἀπήγαγεν
verses.king_james: But the chief captain Lysias came *upon us*, and with great violence took *him* away out of our hands,

verses.latin_vulgate: jubens accusatores ejus ad te venire: a quo poteris ipse judicans, de omnibus istis cognoscere, de quibus nos accusamus eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κελεύσας τούς κατηγόρους αὐτοῦ ἔρχεσθαι ἐπί σέ παρ᾽ οὗ δυνήσῃ αὐτὸς ἀνακρίνας περὶ πάντων τούτων ἐπιγνῶναι ὧν ἡμεῖς κατηγοροῦμεν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Commanding his accusers to come unto thee: by examining of whom thyself mayest take knowledge of all these things, whereof we accuse him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adjecerunt autem et Judæi, dicentes hæc ita se habere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνέθεντο δὲ καὶ οἱ ἰουδαῖοι φάσκοντες ταῦτα οὕτως ἔχειν
verses.king_james: And the Jews also assented, saying that these things were so.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit autem Paulus (annuente sibi præside dicere): Ex multis annis te esse judicem genti huic sciens, bono animo pro me satisfaciam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη δέ ὁ παῦλος νεύσαντος αὐτῷ τοῦ ἡγεμόνος λέγειν ἐκ πολλῶν ἐτῶν ὄντα σε κριτὴν τῷ ἔθνει τούτῳ ἐπιστάμενος εὐθύμοτερον τὰ περὶ ἐμαυτοῦ ἀπολογοῦμαι
verses.king_james: Then Paul, after that the governor had beckoned unto him to speak, answered, Forasmuch as I know that thou hast been of many years a judge unto this nation, I do the more cheerfully answer for myself:

verses.latin_vulgate: Potes enim cognoscere quia non plus sunt mihi dies quam duodecim, ex quo ascendi adorare in Jerusalem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δυναμένου σου γνῶναι ὅτι οὐ πλείους εἰσίν μοι ἡμέραι ἤ δεκαδύο, ἀφ᾽ ἡς ἀνέβην προσκυνήσων ἐν ἰερουσαλήμ
verses.king_james: Because that thou mayest understand, that there are yet but twelve days since I went up to Jerusalem for to worship.

verses.latin_vulgate: et neque in templo invenerunt me cum aliquo disputantem, aut concursum facientem turbæ, neque in synagogis, neque in civitate:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οὔτε ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ εὗρόν με πρός τινα διαλεγόμενον ἢ ἐπισύστασιν ποιοῦντα ὄχλου οὔτε ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς οὔτε κατὰ τὴν πόλιν
verses.king_james: And they neither found me in the temple disputing with any man, neither raising up the people, neither in the synagogues, nor in the city:

verses.latin_vulgate: neque probare possunt tibi de quibus nunc me accusant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὔτε παραστῆσαι με δύνανταί περὶ ὧν νῦν κατηγοροῦσίν μου
verses.king_james: Neither can they prove the things whereof they now accuse me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Confiteor autem hoc tibi, quod secundum sectam quam dicunt hæresim, sic deservio Patri et Deo meo, credens omnibus quæ in lege et prophetis scripta sunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁμολογῶ δὲ τοῦτό σοι ὅτι κατὰ τὴν ὁδὸν ἣν λέγουσιν αἵρεσιν οὕτως λατρεύω τῷ πατρῴῳ θεῷ πιστεύων πάσιν τοῖς κατὰ τὸν νόμον καὶ τοῖς προφήταις γεγραμμένοις
verses.king_james: But this I confess unto thee, that after the way which they call heresy, so worship I the God of my fathers, believing all things which are written in the law and in the prophets:

verses.latin_vulgate: spem habens in Deum, quam et hi ipsi exspectant, resurrectionem futuram justorum et iniquorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλπίδα ἔχων εἰς τὸν θεόν ἣν καὶ αὐτοὶ οὗτοι προσδέχονται ἀνάστασιν μέλλειν ἔσεσθαι νεκρῶν, δικαίων τε καὶ ἀδίκων
verses.king_james: And have hope toward God, which they themselves also allow, that there shall be a resurrection of the dead, both of the just and unjust.

verses.latin_vulgate: In hoc et ipse studeo sine offendiculo conscientiam habere ad Deum et ad homines semper.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τούτῳ δὲ αὐτὸς ἀσκῶ ἀπρόσκοπον συνείδησιν ἔχειν πρὸς τὸν θεὸν καὶ τοὺς ἀνθρώπους διαπαντός
verses.king_james: And herein do I exercise myself, to have always a conscience void of offence toward God, and *toward* men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post annos autem plures eleemosynas facturus in gentem meam, veni, et oblationes, et vota,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δι᾽ ἐτῶν δὲ πλειόνων παρεγενόμην ἐλεημοσύνας ποιήσων εἰς τὸ ἔθνος μου καὶ προσφοράς
verses.king_james: Now after many years I came to bring alms to my nation, and offerings.

verses.latin_vulgate: in quibus invenerunt me purificatum in templo: non cum turba, neque cum tumultu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν οἷς εὗρόν με ἡγνισμένον ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ οὐ μετὰ ὄχλου οὐδὲ μετὰ θορύβου τινές δέ ἀπό τῆς ἀσίας ἰουδαῖοί
verses.king_james: Whereupon certain Jews from Asia found me purified in the temple, neither with multitude, nor with tumult.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quidam autem ex Asia Judæi, quos oportebat apud te præsto esse, et accusare si quid haberent adversum me:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὓς δεῖ ἐπὶ σοῦ παρεῖναι καὶ κατηγορεῖν εἴ τι ἔχοιεν πρὸς μέ
verses.king_james: Who ought to have been here before thee, and object, if they had ought against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: aut hi ipsi dicant si quid invenerunt in me iniquitatis cum stem in concilio,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ αὐτοὶ οὗτοι εἰπάτωσαν εἴ τί εὗρον ἐν ἐμοὶ ἀδίκημα στάντος μου ἐπὶ τοῦ συνεδρίου
verses.king_james: Or else let these same *here* say, if they have found any evil doing in me, while I stood before the council,

verses.latin_vulgate: nisi de una hac solummodo voce qua clamavi inter eos stans: Quoniam de resurrectione mortuorum ego judicor hodie a vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ περὶ μιᾶς ταύτης φωνῆς ἡς ἔκραξα ἑστὼς ἐν αὐτοῖς ὅτι περὶ ἀναστάσεως νεκρῶν ἐγὼ κρίνομαι σήμερον ὑφ᾽ ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: Except it be for this one voice, that I cried standing among them, Touching the resurrection of the dead I am called in question by you this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: Distulit autem illos Felix, certissime sciens de via hac, dicens: Cum tribunus Lysias descenderit, audiam vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσας δὲ ταῦτα ὁ φῆλιξ ἀνεβάλετο αὐτοὺς ἀκριβέστερον εἰδὼς τὰ περὶ τῆς ὁδοῦ εἴπων, ὅταν λυσίας ὁ χιλίαρχος καταβῇ διαγνώσομαι τὰ καθ᾽ ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: And when Felix heard these things, having more perfect knowledge of *that* way, he deferred them, and said, When Lysias the chief captain shall come down, I will know the uttermost of your matter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jussitque centurioni custodire eum, et habere requiem, nec quemquam de suis prohibere ministrare ei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διαταξάμενος τε τῷ ἑκατοντάρχῃ τηρεῖσθαι τὸν παῦλον, ἔχειν τε ἄνεσιν καὶ μηδένα κωλύειν τῶν ἰδίων αὐτοῦ ὑπηρετεῖν ἢ προσέρχεσθαι αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And he commanded a centurion to keep Paul, and to let *him* have liberty, and that he should forbid none of his acquaintance to minister or come unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post aliquot autem dies veniens Felix cum Drusilla uxore sua, quæ erat Judæa, vocavit Paulum, et audivit ab eo fidem quæ est in Christum Jesum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ ἡμέρας τινὰς παραγενόμενος ὁ φῆλιξ σὺν δρουσίλλῃ τῇ γυναικὶ αὐτοῦ οὔσῃ ἰουδαίᾳ μετεπέμψατο τὸν παῦλον καὶ ἤκουσεν αὐτοῦ περὶ τῆς εἰς χριστὸν πίστεως
verses.king_james: And after certain days, when Felix came with his wife Drusilla, which was a Jewess, he sent for Paul, and heard him concerning the faith in Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Disputante autem illo de justitia, et castitate, et de judicio futuro, tremefactus Felix, respondit: Quod nunc attinet, vade: tempore autem opportuno accersam te:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διαλεγομένου δὲ αὐτοῦ περὶ δικαιοσύνης καὶ ἐγκρατείας καὶ τοῦ κρίματος τοῦ μέλλοντος ἔσεσθαι, ἔμφοβος γενόμενος ὁ φῆλιξ ἀπεκρίθη τὸ νῦν ἔχον πορεύου καιρὸν δὲ μεταλαβὼν μετακαλέσομαί σε
verses.king_james: And as he reasoned of righteousness, temperance, and judgment to come, Felix trembled, and answered, Go thy way for this time; when I have a convenient season, I will call for thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: simul et sperans quod pecunia ei daretur a Paulo, propter quod et frequenter accersens eum, loquebatur cum eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἅμα δὲ καὶ ἐλπίζων ὅτι χρήματα δοθήσεται αὐτῷ ὑπὸ τοῦ παύλου ὅπως λύσῃ αὐτὸν διὸ καὶ πυκνότερον αὐτὸν μεταπεμπόμενος ὡμίλει αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: He hoped also that money should have been given him of Paul, that he might loose him: wherefore he sent for him the oftener, and communed with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Biennio autem expleto, accepit successorem Felix Portium Festum. Volens autem gratiam præstare Judæis Felix, reliquit Paulum vinctum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διετίας δὲ πληρωθείσης ἔλαβεν διάδοχον ὁ φῆλιξ πόρκιον φῆστον θέλων τε χάριτας καταθέσθαι τοῖς ἰουδαίοις ὁ φῆλιξ κατέλιπεν τὸν παῦλον δεδεμένον
verses.king_james: But after two years Porcius Festus came into Felix' room: and Felix, willing to shew the Jews a pleasure, left Paul bound.
